entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Zea mays L., the primary crop studied for its response to flooding

entities.name: WEST AFRICAN SAHEL
entities.description: The geographical region characterized by changing rainfall regimes

entities.name: TEMPORARY FLOODING
entities.description: A condition where land is covered by water for a short period, affecting crop growth

entities.name: SURFACE WATER STAGNATION
entities.description: A condition where water remains on the surface without draining

entities.name: GROWTH
entities.description: The physical development of the maize plant

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall output or yield capacity of the crop

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: SPLIT-SPLIT PLOT DESIGN
entities.description: An experimental layout used to test multiple factors

entities.name: SIX-LEAF STAGE
entities.description: A growth stage of maize denoted as V6

entities.name: TASSELING STAGE
entities.description: A growth stage of maize denoted as VT

entities.name: MILKY STAGE
entities.description: A growth stage of maize denoted as R3

entities.name: OBATANPA
entities.description: A specific cultivar of maize used in the experiments

entities.name: STRESS DAY INDEX
entities.description: A metric used to quantify the penalty on yield under flooding conditions

entities.name: GLOBAL WARMING
entities.description: The long-term heating of Earth's climate system

entities.name: HEAVY RAIN EVENTS
entities.description: Intense precipitation events that lead to flooding

entities.name: EXCESS WATER STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by too much water in the soil or environment

entities.name: FOOD INSECURITY
entities.description: The state of being without reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: OUAGADOUGOU
entities.description: The city in Burkina Faso where the field experiments were conducted

entities.name: BURKINA FASO
entities.description: The country in the West African Sahel where the study took place
